_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of database administration and maintenance.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of relational database management system concepts, including normalization theory, indexing strategies, transaction isolation levels, query execution plans, and performance optimization principles.
comments: DCWF ksa equal WIDAI TF domain. TF-K-003 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AI-T-021 (AI, cosine: 0.568), TF-K-001 (TF, cosine: 0.561).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.5/10 (raw 0.551). Shared vocabulary: [database, knowledge]. DCWF-only terms [administration, maintenance] vs WIDAI-only terms [concepts, execution, indexing, isolation, levels, management] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.910 (P=0.949, R=0.874). P exceeds R by 0.075 — the DCWF element's 6 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 22 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (100.0%). "Knowledge of database administration and maintenance." and "Knowledge of relational database management system concepts, including normaliza" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.678 > STS 0.551 (gap +0.127). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [database, knowledge] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 16.7%. Shared tokens: [database, knowledge]. DCWF-only: [administration, maintenance]. WIDAI-only: [concepts, execution, indexing, isolation, levels, management].

signals.detail: STS 0.551 classified as Equal but closer to Superset threshold. Classification may be over-generous.
Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.127 suggests some vocabulary-driven inflation in candidate selection.
